key_points: Food resources include crops and animals that provide food for humans
Improvement in food resources is necessary to meet the needs of the growing population
Crops are classified into cereals, pulses, oil seeds, fruits and vegetables
Kharif crops are grown during the rainy season while Rabi crops are grown during the winter season
Crop variety improvement helps increase crop yield and resistance to pests and diseases
Manure and fertilizers are used to supply nutrients to plants
Irrigation is the artificial supply of water to crops
Cropping patterns include mixed cropping, intercropping and crop rotation
Crop protection management helps control weeds, pests and diseases
Animal husbandry includes the management of livestock for milk, meat and other products
Fish production includes capture fishing and culture fishing
Bee-keeping or apiculture is the rearing of bees for honey and wax production
formulas: Hybridisation → Crossing genetically different plants to produce improved varieties
Mixed cropping → Growing two or more crops together on the same land
Crop rotation → Growing different crops in succession on the same field
Aquaculture → Farming of fish and other aquatic organisms
Apiculture → Rearing of honey bees for honey production
exam_tips: Remember the difference between Kharif and Rabi crops
Understand advantages of mixed cropping, intercropping and crop rotation
Know the difference between manure and fertilizers
Remember key animal husbandry activities such as cattle farming and poultry farming
Learn the meaning of aquaculture and apiculture
Focus on methods used for crop protection and weed control
